channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: A joke petition seeks to help Denmark buy California as Trump eyes Greenland
item.description: Is California for sale? A lighthearted petition to buy the state of California for Denmark prompted more than 200,000 signatures by Wednesday in response to President Donald Trump’s talk about taking control of Greenland, a vast and mineral-rich Arctic island.
item.dc:creator: STEFANIE DAZIO, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Plane caught on camera crashing into New England neighborhood, narrowly missing homes
item.description: A plane crashed in a small wooded area in New Hampshire Tuesday morning. It was caught on camera, showing the plane getting dangerously close to houses.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White House says it has the right to punish AP reporters over Gulf naming dispute
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The White House said Wednesday that news organizations that refuse to use President Donald Trump’s new name for the Gulf of Mexico were telling “lies” and insisted it would continue to bar Associated Press journalists from presidential events.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Early look: The views and venues of the 2026 Winter Olympics
item.description: The 2026 Winter Games will bring the world's best athletes to Northern Italy, with Milan and Cortina as key host cities, marking the first time in 20 years that the games return to Italy.
item.dc:creator: Alyssa Orange, KRON4

item.title: US eggs prices hit a record high of $4.95 and are likely to keep climbing
item.description: OMAHA, Neb. (AP) — Egg prices hit a record high as the U.S. contends with an ongoing bird flu outbreak, but consumers didn't need government figures released Wednesday to tell them eggs are terribly expensive and hard to find at times because of an ongoing.
item.dc:creator: JOSH FUNK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Your closed Party City may soon turn into one of these familiar stores
item.description: What used to be your local Party City store may soon be selling books, recliner chairs, cowboy boots or discount merchandise.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Could obesity drugs help with alcohol cravings?: new study
item.description: Medications that have transformed the treatment of obesity may also help people drink less alcohol, according to new government-funded research.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Navy crew members eject from their jet before it crashes off the San Diego coast
item.description: Authorities say two crew members ejected from a U.S. Navy jet before it crashed off the coast of San Diego and were rescued by a fishing boat.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Military plane crashes near San Diego: authorities
item.description: Emergency crews are responding to reports of a military plane that crashed into the San Diego Bay near Shelter Island, according to the San Diego Fire-Rescue Department Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: Rhea Caoile, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Lawyers for Sonya Massey's family say $10M settlement 'first step;' await trial, legislative changes
item.description: SPRINGFIELD, Ill. (AP) — A $10 million settlement between county officials in Springfield, Illinois, and the family of Sonya Massey is the “first step in getting full justice” for the Black woman fatally shot in her home last summer by a sheriff's deputy, lawyers said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: JOHN O'CONNOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Baltimore sues Glock over handguns that can easily be converted to automatic weapons
item.description: BALTIMORE (AP) — Baltimore is suing gunmaker Glock over design features that allow its handguns to be easily modified to become fully automatic weapons, which enables criminals to inflict more damage and makes the city more dangerous, attorneys argue in a complaint filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: LEA SKENE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: As DOGE hammers away at the US government, Republicans stir with quiet objections
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Republican Sen. Katie Britt has been working to make sure the Trump administration's Department of Government Efficiency doesn't hit what she called “life-saving, groundbreaking research at high-achieving institutions,” including her state's beloved University of Alabama.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO and KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate tees up final vote on RFK Jr. to lead HHS
item.description: The 53-47 vote came as Democrats have been forcing Republicans to use up all debate time on President Trump’s most controversial nominees.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, NewsNation

item.title: Coca-Cola ICE raids? Company faces backlash in face of unfounded rumors
item.description: (KRON) -- A significant backlash has emerged against soft drink manufacturer Coca-Cola in the wake of rumors that the company laid off its own immigrant employees and then called the U.S. Immigration and Customs and Enforcement Agency on them. Rumors have circulated on X, Reddit, and Facebook.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: Federal appeals court upholds singer R. Kelly's convictions, 30-year prison term
item.description: R. Kelly’s racketeering and sex trafficking convictions, along with a 30-year prison sentence, were upheld Wednesday by a federal appeals court that concluded the singer exploited his fame for over a quarter century to sexually abuse girls and young women.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: List: The more than 500 JOANN stores expected to close amid second bankruptcy filing
item.description: When JOANN filed for bankruptcy last month, the company said it expected all of its stores to remain open.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: 2 homeless children freeze to death while sleeping in van with family in Detroit: police
item.description: Police in Detroit said that two homeless children froze to death while they slept in a van with their family on Monday, while the city’s mayor revealed that their mother had reached out to homeless services a few months ago.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Government watchdogs fired by Trump sue his administration and ask a judge to reinstate them
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Eight government watchdogs have sued over their mass firing that removed oversight of President Donald Trump's new administration.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it found 2,400 new JFK assassination records
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The FBI on Tuesday said it discovered 2,400 new records related to the assassination of President John F. Kennedy as federal agencies work to comply with President Donald Trump's executive order last month to release thousands of files.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard to serve as nation’s top intelligence chief
item.description: The Senate voted largely along party lines Wednesday to confirm Tulsi Gabbard to serve as the director of national intelligence, the nation’s top intelligence official, despite strong objections from Democrats and initial misgivings from Republicans who questioned her experience and judgment.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: California city passes controversial homeless camping ban
item.description: The ordinance, which allows the city to fine those who help the homeless and makes it illegal for homeless people to camp on public property, was approved in a 6-1 vote after more than two hours of public comments.
item.dc:creator: Joey Horta, Fox 5

item.title: WATCH: School gym roof collapses under 350 tons of snow
item.description: A recent snowstorm that pummeled parts of Oregon caused the roof at a high school gym to collapse under the weight of about 700,000 pounds of snow, school officials said.
item.dc:creator: Tim Steele, Fox 5

item.title: Christian aid groups weigh life-threatening choices about who to help after USAID funding pause
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — In a warehouse in Haiti, nearly four metric tons of seeds cannot be distributed. Soon the planting season will be gone and with it, the best chance for those seeds to produce emergency food.
item.dc:creator: TIFFANY STANLEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Italy prepares scenic, historic Olympic venues with games now one year away
item.description: This marks the first time Milan will host the Winter Olympics, with the opening ceremony scheduled at San Siro Stadium. The games will feature events in multiple locations, including hockey in a new arena in Milan and various alpine events in Cortina.
item.dc:creator: Matt Barnes, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Monty the giant schnauzer wins Westminster dog show
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — This time, the giant schnauzer went the full Monty. After coming close in the last two years, Monty the giant schnauzer won the top prize at the Westminster Kennel Club dog show Tuesday night, leaving handler and co-owner Katie Bernardin almost too emotional to speak.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Want to send video of elephant pooping to your ex for V-day? Here's how
item.description: Want to "name a turd after a turd" for Valentine's Day? The Memphis Zoo has you covered by sending a video to your ex or your least favorite person.
item.dc:creator: Deja Davis, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Audio from Titan submersible implosion released by NOAA
item.description: In the 23-second recording, the sound of static can be heard, followed by a booming noise — then more static.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Felony charges filed against man accused of killing pregnant woman in Portage Park
item.description: A man accused of fatally shooting a pregnant woman whose premature child later died is now facing felony charges.
item.dc:creator: Gabriel Castillo, NewsNation

item.title: Gangs not only ones preying on migrant women, boys
item.description: The road from Central America to the U.S. border is paved with dangers for vulnerable migrants often traveling with children.
item.dc:creator: Julian Resendiz, NewsNation

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense sounds alarm on unidentified blood at student murders home
item.description: University of Idaho student murder suspect Bryan Kohberger's defense is arguing that two blood samples from unidentified males at the crime scene could clear him.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Capitol Police: Ex-aide to Trump's labor secretary nominee 'bypassed' security at congressional building
item.description: A former aide to former U.S. Rep. Lori Chavez-DeRemer “bypassed" a security screening yesterday at a congressional office building, U.S. Capitol Police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tren de Aragua crackdown: 8 Venezuelan gang members indicted for sex trafficking
item.description: The U.S. Attorney's Office and law enforcement agencies in Tennessee announced the indictment of multiple people in the state with ties to the violent Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Disney, amid changes to DEI initiatives, will alter content warning ahead of 'Peter Pan,' 'Dumbo' and other older films
item.description: Amid the changes, Disney is also replacing the language in the content advisory disclaimers that auto-play before certain older films streaming on Disney+.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, NewsNation

item.title: Musk defends DOGE alongside Trump in striking Oval Office moment
item.description: Elon Musk on Tuesday defended the work of the Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) alongside President Trump, who invited Musk to speak and sat at the resolute desk as the tech mogul gave a lengthy address.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer in fatal collision at Arizona airport
item.description: One person was killed and others were injured when a private jet owned by Mötley Crüe singer Vince Neil collided with another jet Monday afternoon at the Scottsdale Airport in Arizona.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump sued after quietly firing top whistleblower protector
item.description: President Trump late Friday fired the leader of an office designed to protect government whistleblowers, a detail revealed in a Monday lawsuit from the head of the Office of the Special Counsel (OSC).
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WGN 9
